title: Merrion Square (The Rocky Road To Dublin)
content:
Grey clouds on the tinted sky,
        A drifting moon, a quiet breeze
        Drooping mournfully to cry
        In the branches of the trees.

        The crying wind, the sighing trees,
        The ruffled stars, the darkness falling
        Down the sky, and on the breeze
        A belated linnet calling.
meaning:
James Stephens's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Merrion Square (The Rocky Road To Dublin)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
